content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Let's call a contiguous subsequence of AAA a segment.

A segment is good if it can be divided into at most KKK segments such that the sum of elements in each of these sub-segments is at most SSS.

You need to find the maximum number of elements in a good segment.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and SSS.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum length of a good segment.
constraints: 1≤Ai≤S1 \le A_i \le S1≤Ai​≤S for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: One of the segments with length 444 is [1,3,2,1][1, 3, 2, 1][1,3,2,1]. It can be divided into two segments [1,3][1, 3][1,3] and [2,1][2, 1][2,1] whose sums are at most S=5S = 5S=5.
